content: Language learning vocabulary acquisition for the term 'reflexion' itself can be used as a grounded analogy to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as human learners solidify their understanding of 'reflexion' by repeatedly encountering, using, and reflecting on the term across different contexts, LLMs can iteratively reinforce their self-reflection capability by grounding abstract self-improvement loops in concrete linguistic and contextual examples of reflexive practice drawn from human educational materials. This creates a bidirectional training loop where human pedagogical framing of reflection improves AI's ability to perform self-reflection.
